title: Topics in Health Informatics for Health/Life Sciences Students
description: Basic literacy in health informatics. Innovative approaches to healthcare implementation, access to care, and data utilization.

title: Quantitative Methods in Biochemistry
description: Quantitative and computational methods of biological information transfer. Methods, data analysis approaches, and theories involved in bioinformatics, computational biology, and high-throughput data.

title: Nucleic Acids-Structure and Function
description: Chemical, physical and biological properties of nucleic acids and their role in replication, transcription, translation and regulation of expression of genetic material.

title: Biomathematics
description: Uses of mathematics in the biological sciences; experimental design and modelling of biological processes.

title: Survey of Algae
description: Algae, considering their morphology, life history, classification, and ecology.

title: Experimental Biology of Invertebrates
description: Behaviour and ecology of invertebrates as revealed by hands-on experiments.

title: Molecular Genetics
description: Isolation and identification of genes, analysis of gene structure; gene expression and regulation.

title: Fundamentals of Evolutionary Biology
description: Natural selection; population genetics, quantitative genetics and systematics; classical and molecular approaches.

title: Ecological Methodology
description: Design, execution, and analysis of ecological surveys and experiments, with field and computer techniques.

title: Population Genetics
description: Theoretical and experimental aspects of population and quantitative genetics.

title: Zoogeography
description: Role of physical, ecological, and evolutionary processes in determining the geographic distribution of animals.

title: Fundamentals of Biochemistry
description: Amino acids and proteins. Enzymes and enzymology. Human metabolic pathways. Structure, function and metabolism of carbohydrates including glycogen storage and gluconeogenesis.

title: Fundamentals of Cell Biology
description: Structure and function of plant and animal cells; membrane models, cytoplasmic organelles, biological information from gene to protein.

title: Introduction to Organic Chemistry
description: Structure, bonding and physical properties of aliphatic and aromatic compounds; mechanistic analysis of chemical reactivity.

title: Thermodynamics, Kinetics and Organic Chemistry
description: Fundamentals of chemical reactivity: thermodynamics; kinetics; organic chemistry, including stereochemistry. (Credit is granted for only one of CHEM 113 or CHEM 123.)

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Spectroscopy of organic compounds. Mechanistic analysis of chemical reactivity of common functional groups.

title: Introduction to Organic Chemistry
description: Structure, bonding and physical properties of aliphatic and aromatic compounds; mechanistic analysis of chemical reactivity.

title: Thermodynamics, Kinetics and Organic Chemistry
description: Fundamentals of chemical reactivity: thermodynamics; kinetics; organic chemistry, including stereochemistry.

title: Structure and Bonding in Chemistry
description: Fundamentals of bonding theories and structural chemistry, with applications relevant to modern society.

title: Molecular Biochemistry
description: Structure, function and metabolism of lipids, steroids, amino acids and nucleotides; the biochemistry and molecular biology of replication, transcription, translation and gene regulation.

title: Comparative Invertebrate Zoology
description: An introduction to the unity, diversity and evolutionary history of invertebrates.

title: Genetics, Evolution and Ecology
description: Principles of storage and transmission of genetic variation; origin and evolution of species and their ecological interactions.

title: Genetics for Life
description: How genetic differences determine individual characteristics, and how they are inherited, analyzed, and modified.

title: Biology of the Cell
description: The principles of cellular and molecular biology using bacterial and eukaryotic examples.

title: Fundamentals of Ecology
description: Dynamics of plant and animal populations, structure of ecological communities and functioning of ecosystems.

title: Elementary Statistics for Applications
description: Classical, nonparametric, and robust inferences about means, variances, and analysis of variance, using computers. Emphasis on problem formulation, assumptions, and interpretation.

title: Differential Calculus with Applications
description: Derivatives of elementary functions. Applications and modelling: graphing, optimization.
